decisions.decision: Keep the step-5 frontend regression slice focused on `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts`, `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx`, `frontend/src/__tests__/System.test.tsx`, and the `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx` path that renders `BayesNetGraph`. Treat `frontend/src/__tests__/Portfolio.test.tsx` only as optional spillover cleanup if shared fixtures or types need adjustment, not as a required opt-in behavior suite.
alternatives_rejected: Expand the required refresh to `Portfolio.test.tsx` even though it stays on the default `useMarkets()` path and does not freeze the opt-in contract.
Introduce a new standalone `BayesNetGraph` suite for this step instead of extending the existing MarketDetail seam.

decisions.decision: Preserve the public `listMarkets(...)` client contract in `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts` by covering both the legacy string shorthand and the object filter shape, including the normalized `status=resolved` plus `include_resolved=true` wire behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Narrow the public client contract to object filters only as part of this test refresh.
Refresh the client tests without asserting the resolved-status normalization behavior.

decisions.decision: Strengthen the route/component refresh by pairing request-shape assertions with rendered behavior checks, and by using fixtures whose `meta.filters` stay consistent with the returned rows so the tests cannot pass with contradictory resolved-market data.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only the `listMarkets(...)` arguments and ignore whether the rendered output changes when resolved rows are hidden or included.
Rely on render-only assertions with permissive mocks that do not prove the new opt-in request shape.
Keep lightweight but contradictory fixtures that return resolved rows while claiming `include_resolved: false`.

decisions.decision: Use a focused verification matrix: rerun only the named `/v1/markets` backend unit tests for `include_resolved` filtering and run Vitest only for `api-client`, `MarketList`, `System`, and `MarketDetail` rather than broad backend or frontend suites.
alternatives_rejected: Rerun the full `BayesMarketApiUnitTests` class or the whole backend test module.
Run the entire frontend test suite instead of the four affected files.

steps.task: Refresh `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts` so the market-list client contract stays explicit: default `/v1/markets`, legacy string shorthand (`listMarkets("active")`), object filters, `includeResolved` snake-case serialization, and the normalized `status: "resolved"` plus `include_resolved=true` request path all remain frozen by test coverage.

Rework `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx` around contract-consistent fixtures and request-aware mocks so the suite proves both halves of the new default: the initial all-status view calls `listMarkets(undefined)` and hides resolved rows, while explicit status selections call `{ status: ... }` and render the matching active or resolved collection.

Refresh `frontend/src/__tests__/System.test.tsx` to assert the explicit `{ includeResolved: true }` opt-in and verify the rendered aggregate counts/stats with fixtures that include both active and resolved markets, keeping `meta.filters.include_resolved` aligned with the returned collection.

Extend `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx` at the existing MarketDetail seam so the graph path proves it requests the resolved-inclusive collection via `listMarkets({ includeResolved: true })` and still renders correctly when the fixture includes the focus market alongside resolved nodes; only touch `Portfolio.test.tsx` if shared fixture cleanup is needed.

Run the focused verification commands after the frontend refresh: the named `/v1/markets` methods in `tests.test_bayes_market_api.BayesMarketApiUnitTests` that cover default exclusion, explicit inclusion, resolved-status normalization, and invalid `include_resolved` inputs, plus `pnpm test -- src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx src/__tests__/System.test.tsx src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx` from `frontend/`.
